channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: New video leak may have revealed the full Nothing Phone 3a and Phone 3a Pro design
item.description: Leaked promo videos for the upcoming Nothing phones have appeared online, telling us more about the handsets.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: This is what they never told you about online privacy
item.description: Understanding the language of the internet can help you protect your personal information from bad actors. Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A data center in every home! Energy company wants to heat your water for (almost) free but there's a catch
item.description: A British Gas-backed trial will see waste heat from data centers used to deliver free hot water to UK homes.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Musk: Federal workers who gave 'good responses' to new policy deserve promotion consideration
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said he’s encouraged by the “good responses” he’s received so far to an email asking federal employees to detail their recent work efforts and suggested the employees who sent them be considered for promotion. “A large number of good responses have been received already.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Nvidia confirms 'rare issue' with some RTX 5090 and RTX 5070 Ti GPUs – here’s how to check if you’re affected and to get a replacement
item.description: The launch of Nvidia's new GPUs has gone badly enough already, but now there's a hardware-level glitch slowing down RTX 5090 and 5070 Ti GPUs.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: This slick new service puts ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Wikipedia on the map
item.description: I don’t know about you, but I tend to think about my favorite tech tools as being split into two separate saucepans: the “classic” apps we’ve known and relied on for ages and then the newer “AI” apps that have shown up over the past several months to serve some super-specific purpose.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: Apple’s hidden white noise feature may be just the productivity boost you need
item.description: As I write this, the most pleasing sound is washing over me—gentle waves ebbing and flowing onto the shore. Sadly, I’m not actually on some magnificent tropical beach. Instead, the sounds of the sea are being generated by my Mac.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: This is what you are doing wrong when scammers call
item.description: The line between spam and scams is thin. Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson details his tips for identifying and avoiding phone scams, robocalls and unwanted solicitations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to set your preferred web browser on your desktop and mobile devices
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy takes you through the steps to easily set your preferred web browser whether you're using a Mac, Windows PC, iPhone or Android.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brazil Supreme Court justice orders Rumble suspension nationwide for alleged non-compliance
item.description: A Brazilian Supreme Court justice has ordered the suspension of video-sharing platform Rumble in the South American nation for allegedly refusing to comply with court orders.
item.dc:creator: Mauricio Savarese, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Amazon just overtook Walmart in revenue for the first time
item.description: Amazon overtakes Walmart in terms of quarterly revenue, but how long will it last before it regains pole position?
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Sweden is investigating a damaged cable in the Baltic Sea
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Swedish authorities said on Friday they were investigating a damaged cable that was discovered in the Baltic Sea, the latest in a string of recent incidents of ruptured undersea cables that have heightened fears of Russian sabotage and spying in the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: US Marshals Service deputizes Musk's security team: reports
item.description: The U.S. Marshals Service has deputized some of tech billionaire Elon Musk's private security detail, giving them specific rights and protections of federal law enforcement agents, multiple outlets reported Friday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Apple drops encryption feature for UK users after government reportedly demanded backdoor access
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple said Friday it will stop offering an advanced data security option for British users after the government reportedly demanded that the company provide backdoor access for any data those users have stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Substack bets big on video as TikTok’s future remains uncertain
item.description: With TikTok’s future in the U.S. still uncertain, Substack is doubling down on attracting video creators. As of yesterday, creators can now publish video posts directly from the Substack app—a feature previously limited to desktop.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Everything new on Max in March 2024
item.description: Here's the full list of everything new on Max in March 2025 – including new movie releases Sing Sing and Heretic.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Climate change is shrinking glaciers faster than ever, with 7 trillion tons lost since 2000
item.description: A new and massive study finds that climate change is melting the world’s mountain glaciers faster than ever.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bannon: ‘Oligarchs’ will abandon Republicans
item.description: Steve Bannon argued that the “oligarchs” close to President Trump will abandon Republicans the same way they did to Democrats. Bannon was briefly interviewed by CNN’s Donie O’Sullivan on Wednesday, when he continued his case against tech billionaires Elon Musk and Mark Zuckerberg.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Ex-OpenAI CTO Mira Murati launches AI startup, recruits top talent from rivals
item.description: Former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati has launched an AI startup, Thinking Machines Lab, escalating the fight for top talent and reshaping competition in the fast-evolving sector.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Elon Musk finally reveals 'why Memphis' for xAI supercomputer
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. -- Months after xAI put its operations in Memphis, Elon Musk finally announced why he chose the Bluff City to host the world's largest artificial intelligence supercomputer.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, NewsNation

item.title: iPhones may soon be able to use satellite connections indoors
item.description: Sending messages via satellite on iPhones should soon be as easy to as using your mobile network signal, as Apple and satellite partner Globalstar put a new satellite constellation into space.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft’s quantum ambition: One million qubits in the palm of your hand
item.description: Microsoft announced a breakthrough in quantum computing today, unveiling a new kind of quantum processing unit, using a new type of material, to create what it says is a “radically different type of qubit.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Aryaka adds AI-powered observability to SASE platform
item.description: SASE vendor Aryaka is looking to alleviate network alert fatigue with a little help from AI. Today Aryaka announced its Winter 2025 platform update, introducing its new AI Observe tool for advanced threat detection and prevention.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Intro to Elixir: A fresh take on functional programming
item.description: Elixir is one of the most interesting stories in the world of programming languages right now. Built on the Erlang ecosystem, Elixir offers a fresh take on functional programming and new ways of thinking about concurrency and fault tolerance.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Experts give up hope for 157 false killer whales stranded on a beach in Australia's Tasmania state
item.description: Marine experts have given up hope of rescuing more than 150 false killer whales that stranded on a remote beach on Australia’s island state of Tasmania.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How drones may be a big help to first responders in the future
item.description: A recent challenge sponsored by NIST tasked drone designers with creating a system to help first responders map indoor environments. The post How drones may be a big help to first responders in the future first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Data center spending to top $1 trillion by 2029 as AI transforms infrastructure
item.description: Massive global demand for AI technology is causing data centers to increase spending on servers, power, and cooling infrastructure. As a result, data center CapEx spending will hit $1.1 trillion by 2029, up from $430 billion in 2024, according to Dell’Oro Group. Much of this growth is due to AI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 3 key features of Postman’s AI Agent Builder
item.description: The software landscape is shifting from passive business processes to dynamic, AI-driven workflows. AI agents—systems that interact with APIs, make decisions, and execute complex tasks—are at the forefront of this transformation.
item.dc:creator: Info World
